data.title: HSBC names insider Pam Kaur as first female CFO - report
data.description:
HSBC (NYSE:HSBC) appointed Pam Kaur as its first female Chief Financial Officer, replacing Georges Elhedery, who was promoted to CEO earlier.

Kaur, 60, currently is the chief risk and compliance officer at HSBC (HSBC) and joined the bank in April 2013 as group head of internal audit.

She brings extensive experience from her previous roles at major financial institutions such as Citigroup and Deutsche Bank.

This decision marks a milestone in the bank's 160-year history and is part of a restructuring plan that will take effect on January 1, 2025. The bank will reorganize its operations into four distinct business lines: Hong Kong, UK, corporate, and institutional banking.

“The new structure will result in a simpler, more dynamic, and agile organisation as we focus on executing against our strategic priorities, which remain unchanged,” CEO Elhedery said in a statement, according to the report.

Jon Bingham, the interim Group CFO, will resume his role of global financial controller.

data.title: Wintrust Financial GAAP EPS of $2.47 misses by $0.01, revenue of $615.73M misses by $20.94M
data.description:
• Wintrust Financial press release (NASDAQ:WTFC): Q3 GAAP EPS of $2.47 misses by $0.01.
  • Revenue of $615.73M (+7.1% Y/Y) misses by $20.94M.
  • Pre-tax, pre-provision income totaled $255.0 million as compared to $251.4 million for the second quarter of 2024.
  • "We expect net interest margin to remain in the 3.50% range in the fourth quarter of 2024 and into 2025."
  • Provision for credit losses totaled $22.3 million in the third quarter of 2024, including a one-time acquisition-related Day 1 provision of approximately $15.5 million, as compared to a provision for credit losses of $40.1 million in the second quarter of 2024.
  • Tangible book value per common share increased to $76.15 as of September 30, 2024, as compared to $72.01 as of June 30, 2024.

data.title: Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacifico GAAP EPADS of $3.13, revenue of Ps.8.23B
data.description:
• Grupo Aeroportuario del Pacifico press release (NYSE:PAC): Q3 GAAP EPADS of $3.13.
  • Revenue of Ps.8.23B (+11.4% Y/Y).
  • EBITDA increased by Ps. 237.8 million, or 5.6%, from Ps. 4,629.9 million in 3Q23 to Ps. 4,507.6 million in 3Q24.
  • The company reports a financial position of cash and cash equivalents as of September 30, 2024, of Ps. 15,828.0 million.
  • During 3Q24, total passengers at the company’s 14 airports decreased by 923.2 thousand passengers, a decrease of 5.7%, compared to 3Q23.

data.title: Axis to sell auto finance business to Canada's Fionic
data.description:
• Axis Auto Finance (TSX:AXIS:CA) and its operating subsidiaries involved in the company's auto finance business have entered into an asset purchase agreement with Fionic Canada regarding their acquisition of the auto loan assets and undertaking of Axis through the acquisition of certain assets and assumption of certain liabilities and its subsidiaries relating to the Axis Auto Finance business.
  • The base purchase price for the assets is approximately $114M.
  • Fionic is an arm's length party to Axis and its affiliates and the transaction was negotiated on an arm's length basis.
  • The transaction will require the approval of two-thirds of votes cast by shareholders of the company at a meeting expected to be held in early December.

data.description:
• Independent Bank (NASDAQ:IBTX) declares $0.38/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.

data.title: HUHUTECH prices IPO at $4 per share
data.description:
• HUHUTECH International (HUHU) priced its initial public offering of 1.05M shares at a public offering price of $4.0 per share.
  • The ordinary shares have been approved for listing on the Nasdaq Capital Market and are expected to commence trading on October 22, 2024, under the ticker symbol “HUHU.”
  • The aggregate gross proceeds expected of $4.2M.
  • Underwriters have a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional ~157K ordinary shares.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about October 23, 2024.
  • HUHUTECH (HUHU) International Group is a professional provider of factory facility management and monitoring systems.

data.title: Asia-Pacific markets mixed, tracking global market sentiment, eyes on Hyundai India market debut
data.description:
Asia-Pacific markets trade mixed on Tuesday, taking cues from a weak lead on Wall Street overnight, as Treasury yields surged on signs of economic resilience and fears of a reacceleration of inflation in the U.S.

All eyes are on India’s markets, where Hyundai India is set to debut after a 278.56 billion rupee ($3.3 billion) IPO, the nation’s largest ever.

The Japan (NKY:IND) fall -1.44% to around 38,600 while the broader Topix Index lost 0.4% to 2,670 on Tuesday, hitting their lowest levels in over two weeks. The Japanese yen depreciated toward 151 per dollar on Tuesday.

In Japan, investors also turned cautious amid political uncertainty in the lead up to Japan’s general election this weekend, while awaiting a slew of corporate earnings reports.

China (SHCOMP) rose +0.53% to around 3,280 while the Shenzhen Component gained 0.4% to 10,520 on Tuesday, with mainland stocks rising for the third straight session as investors continued to assess the impact of stimulus measures in China.

On Monday, the People’s Bank of China lowered both its one- and five-year loan prime rates by 25 basis points as part of a broader stimulus package to revive the economy.

Hong Kong (HSI) rose +0.61% to 20,550 on Tuesday morning, attempting to recover from a steep decline the day before, with gains mainly from tech and financials.

India (SENSEX) rose +0.20%

Australia (AS51) fall -1.54% to around 8,260 on Tuesday, hitting a one-week low. Investors look forward to the latest Australian PMI reports to gauge the strength of the economy and guide the outlook for monetary policy.

In the U.S., on Monday, all three major indexes ended mixed with the Dow and S&P 500 retreating from all-time closing highs, after their longest weekly rally of the year, as investors braced for another week packed earnings while Treasury yields surged further, pushing consumer and home builder stocks down.

U.S. stock futures were little changed on Tuesday as the third quarter earnings season picks up steam: Dow -0.13%; S&P 500 -0.16%; Nasdaq -0.25%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia:

Japan's inflation rate slows to 2.5% in September

China's Q3 GDP figures point at a slowing economy

Australia's unemployment rate steady at 4.1%

Japan records higher than expected deficit, exports drop first time in 10 months

data.title: Logitech Non-GAAP EPS of $1.20, revenue of $1.12B, raises guidance
data.description:
• Logitech press release (NASDAQ:LOGI): Q2 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.20.
  • Revenue of $1.12B (+5.7% Y/Y).
  • GAAP gross margin was 43.6 percent, up 210 basis points compared to Q2 of the prior year.

  • Logitech raised its full-year outlook for Fiscal Year 2025:

    Previous FY25 outlook

    New FY25 outlook

    Sales

    $4.34 - $4.43 billion

    $4.39 - $4.47 billion

    Sales growth (in US dollars, year over year)

    1% - 3%

    2% - 4%

    Non-GAAP operating income

    $700 - $730 million

    $720 - $750 million

    Non-GAAP op. inc. growth (year over year)

    0% - 4%

    3% - 7%

data.title: Tuesday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
10:00 AM Fed’s Harker: “Fintech”
Philadelphia Federal Reserve Bank President Patrick Harker to speak on "Fintech" before the Eighth Annual Fintech Conference hosted by the Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia.
10:00 AM Richmond Fed Mfg. Index
The Richmond Manufacturing Index rates the relative level of business conditions among manufacturers including shipments, new orders, and employment. The October Richmond Manufacturing Index is expected to increase modestly to -15 after reporting sluggish activity in September. The composite manufacturing index edged down from −19 in August to −21 in September.
11:00 AM Treasury Buyback Announcement (Preliminary)
The Treasury conducts buybacks to improve liquidity for certain maturities, limit variations in auction sizes, and reduce maturity peaks in outstanding debt.
1:00 PM Money Supply
The money supply is the total amount of money—cash, coins, and balances in bank accounts—in circulation.
Click here to see the weekly economic calendar.

data.title: Mangoceuticals refutes claims made by Lilly in weight-loss drug lawsuit
data.description:
Mangoceuticals (NASDAQ:MGRX), which operates the men’s health platform MangoRx, said it strongly refutes any and all claims made in a lawsuit filed against the company by Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) over a compounded version of Lilly’s popular weight-loss drug tirzepatide.

Lilly has accused MangoRx (MGRX) of improperly marketing an oral formulation of tirzepatide. Lilly (LLY) markets injected formulations of tirzepatide under the brand names Mounjaro and Zepbound.

“MangoRx (MGRX) believes it has strong arguments against Eli Lilly’s claims and intends to vigorously defend itself in this matter,” Mangoceuticals said in a statement.

Earlier Monday, Reuters reported that Lilly had filed lawsuits against three companies over the marketing of compounded versions of tirzepatide. The three companies are Pivotal Peptides, MangoRx (MGRX) and Genesis Lifestyle Medicine.

Mangoceuticals (MGRX) stock rallied in May after the company announced it planned to sell proprietary oral versions of Lilly's (LLY) tirzepatide and Novo Nordisk's (NVO) semaglutide, which is sold under the brand names Ozempic and Wegovy.

Mangoceuticals (MGRX) held its initial public offering in March 2023.
